securityCenter.heroSubtitle: Privacy is your first line of defense.
securityCenter.heroDesc: Alpha Chat isn't a chat app with encryption bolted on. It's a security architecture with a messaging app on top. Every feature is built to protect you, not to collect your data.
securityCenter.whyText1: Alpha Chat wasn't built to be "just another chat".
securityCenter.whyText2: It was built to protect digital freedom.
securityCenter.whyText3b: It's the architecture of the app.
securityCenter.finalSubmotto: Privacy is your first line of defense.
securityCenter.serverKnowsNotItems: Message text|Photos and videos|Audio and documents|Cryptographic keys
securityCenter.phoenixTagline: The ultimate defense. Irreversible by design.
securityCenter.phoenixIntro: The Phoenix Protocol orders the permanent destruction of your account when you have no alternatives. It requires triple verification:
securityCenter.phoenixStep1: Recovery ID — from your physical Recovery Card
securityCenter.phoenixStep3: Email token — single-use link with 15-minute TTL
securityCenter.phoenixScenariosLabel: Real scenarios it was designed for
securityCenter.phoenixDestroyItems: Messages|Media and attachments|Sessions and tokens|Signal keys|Authorized devices|Cache|History
securityCenter.phoenixWarning: ⚠️ This operation is irreversible. Once confirmed, the account cannot be recovered.

e2e.title: End-to-End Encryption
e2e.p1before: Every message is encrypted on your device
e2e.p1after: being sent. The server never receives plaintext — it only receives encrypted data it cannot read.
e2e.p2: Only you and the recipient hold the keys to decrypt messages. No intermediaries, no possible interception.

x3dh.title: X3DH — The First Handshake
x3dh.p1after: is the protocol Alpha Chat uses to establish a secure session between two users who have never spoken before.
x3dh.p2: Cryptographic keys are exchanged so that no key ever travels in plaintext over the network. Even if an attacker intercepted all packets, they couldn't extract anything useful.
x3dh.p3: The result is a shared secret, known only to the two participants, used as the basis for the Double Ratchet.

ratchet.title: Double Ratchet — One Key Per Message
ratchet.p1after: generates a unique cryptographic key for every single message. The key is used once and then deleted.
ratchet.forwardSecrecyDesc: Even if a future key were compromised, previous messages remain safe.
ratchet.postCompromiseDesc: After a compromise, the system automatically "heals" itself with subsequent messages.

media.title: Encrypted Media — AES-256-GCM
media.p1before: Photos, videos, audio and documents are encrypted with
media.p1after: directly on your device before upload.
media.p2: The server only sees opaque encrypted blobs. Even with physical database access, no file can be seen, heard, or read.

zk.title: Zero Knowledge
zk.p1: The server knows only the bare minimum needed to deliver messages.

ghost.title: Ghost Mode
ghost.p1: You're as invisible as you want. You can choose to hide every trace of your presence.
ghost.lastSeenDesc: No one sees when you last used the app.
ghost.onlineDesc: You never appear as "online" in the contact list.
ghost.receiptsDesc: Ticks don't show whether you've read the message.

burn.title: Burn After Read
burn.p1before: Some messages must disappear after being read. With
burn.p1after: the message self-destructs as soon as the recipient opens it.
burn.p2: Nothing remains — neither on the recipient's device nor on the server. As if it never existed.

disappearing.title: Disappearing messages
disappearing.p1: Every conversation can have a disappearing timer. After the set time (from 5 seconds to 7 days), messages are automatically deleted from both devices.
disappearing.p2: Ideal for sensitive conversations that shouldn't leave traces over time.

destroy.title: Secure Destroy
destroy.p1after: permanently deletes a message from all devices and from the server.
destroy.p2: Unlike regular deletion — which can leave traces — Secure Destroy removes the message permanently and irreversibly, leaving no sign of its existence in the conversation.

safety.title: Safety Number — Identity Verification
safety.p1after: is a unique cryptographic fingerprint of your Signal session with a contact. By comparing it with your contact, you can verify that no one has intercepted the communication.
safety.mitmDesc: Detects Man-in-the-Middle attacks before they can compromise the conversation.
safety.qrDesc: Scan the contact's QR for instant verification.
safety.fingerprintDesc: Compare the 12 groups of 5 digits in person or over the phone.

deviceSecurity.title: Device security
deviceSecurity.p1: Alpha Chat protects your data from a physical standpoint too. If someone has your phone, they can't open the app without authentication.

emergencyLock.title: Emergency Lock
emergencyLock.p1after: immediately disconnects all your devices and revokes all active sessions. The account remains intact and recoverable.
emergencyLock.hint: Accessible from any browser via the Emergency Portal — even without your phone.

multidevice.title: Multi-Device
multidevice.p1: You can use Alpha Chat on multiple devices simultaneously. Each device establishes an independent Signal session — it doesn't share keys with the others.
multidevice.p2: You can individually revoke any device at any time from the Devices page, without having to change your password or invalidate the others.

block.title: Block users
block.p1: You can block any user at any time. Once blocked, they can no longer send you messages, see your profile, or know you exist on Alpha Chat.
block.p2: Blocking is silent: the blocked user receives no notification.
